calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-23-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ruth Sullivan for and with Vernon White. Carida Johnson in Propria Persona. Antonio Gamez, Jr. for San Joaquin Department of Child Support Services (via court call).

Mr. Gamez, Jr. stated that they have submitted a response and that the lien is still valid. Mr. White reported that he filed his paperwork, that the Judge had stated there would be no arrears, that other counties dismissed the cases and that San Joaquin is the only county to not dismiss their claim and read information to the court off of form FL687. Ms. Sullivan stated that the administrator would like Mr. White to receive his distribution but that he cannot as San Joaquin refuses to dismiss their claim. Mr. Gamez, Jr. explained that there are two methods for collecting the back support, that Mr. White's benefits could not be attached, that it was made clear in court that the lien would not be dismissed and read from section 4E regarding the collection process. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-08-2015 Continued To Apr-22-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Vernon White in Propria Persona.

The tentative ruling on this matter was grant without hearing. Mr. White appeared to make his objection. The court stated that Mr. White would need to file his objections in writing no later than April 15, 2015 and the accounting petition was continued to April 22, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Sep-29-2014 Continued To Oct-20-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (167 Harvard Street, San Francisco)
Off Calendar Aug-27-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ruth Sullivan for and with Vivian White.

The sale was confirmed at $823,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). Proceeds from the sale are to be deposited into a blocked account. Status on filing of the receipt for the blocked account was set for September 29, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (167 Harvard Street, San Francisco)
Off Calendar Aug-04-2014 Continued To Aug-25-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Francisco Gutierrez appearing for Ruth Downs Sullivan counsel for Vivian White.

Counsel reported that the sale has fallen through and that a new buyer is lined up. Counsel will file an amended petition. The petition for sale of real property was continued to August 25, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy McConico. (Not Reported)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Strike Request For Special Notice And Memorandum Of Points And Authority And Authorities In Support Of Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Request For Special Notice And Declaration Of Ruth Downs Sullivan In Support Of Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Request For Special Notice
Off Calendar Mar-12-2014 Continued To Apr-16-14 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Authority To Borrow Money And For A Lien Against Real Property To Secure The Loan
Off Calendar Feb-11-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ruth Sullivan for Vivian White. Vernon White in Propria Persona.

Ms. Sullivan explained that the settlement in the unlawful detainer action was extended until the Court ruled on this petition, that they agreed that Mr. White would receive a certain amount so that he could move and then receive the rest once he has moved. Ms. Sullivan also stated that the body and the prayer in the petition state the amount of interest, that all of the Beneficiaries are aware and have consented, that the note has always indicated a 5% interest rate and agreed that Mr. Neff (Lender) is a stranger to the Parties. Mr. White stated that he was okay with the settlement and that he was all set up to move. The Court granted the petition for authority to borrow money, etc. provided language on the note is revised to include the interest rate (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
